name: NIST Special Publication 800-171
description: Security requirements for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) in nonfederal systems — the foundation of DFARS 252.204-7012 and CMMC for defense contractors. Revision 3 (May 2024) restructures the catalog around SP 800-53r5. Note: DoD continues to assess against Revision 2 until it announces the Rev 3 transition.

highlights: Requirements reduced 110 → 97, but assessment objectives grew 320 → 390 — Rev 3 is more specific and more rigorous, not lighter
Three new families: Planning (PL), System and Services Acquisition (SA), and Supply Chain Risk Management (SR); Security Assessment renamed Security Assessment and Monitoring
SP 800-53r5 becomes the single authoritative source — the basic/derived split and FIPS 200 lineage are gone
88 organization-defined parameters (ODPs) replace vague terms like "periodically"; NFO tailoring category eliminated
New numbering (03.01.01 style) with per-requirement titles; NIST publishes official transition mapping tables
IMPORTANT: DoD continues to assess DFARS 252.204-7012 / CMMC against Revision 2 until it announces otherwise

changes.title: Security requirements reduced from 110 to 97
changes.oldText: 110 security requirements across 14 families, split into basic requirements (from FIPS 200) and derived requirements (from SP 800-53).
changes.newText: 97 security requirements across 17 families, all derived from SP 800-53r5 as the single authoritative source.
changes.rationale: NIST removed outdated and redundant requirements and merged closely related ones into multi-part requirements. Fewer line items does not mean less work: the requirements are more specific, and assessment objectives grew from 320 to 390.

changes.title: Basic vs. derived requirement distinction eliminated
changes.oldText: Basic security requirements were drawn from FIPS 200; derived requirements were drawn from NIST SP 800-53.
changes.rationale: Per the official NIST FAQ: recasting the requirements using only SP 800-53 as the single authoritative source significantly increased specificity and clarity. FIPS 200 is no longer a source.

changes.title: New family: Planning (PL)
changes.newText: Planning (PL) family added, consistent with the SP 800-53B moderate baseline.
changes.rationale: One of three new families added to maintain consistency with the SP 800-53B moderate control baseline. Covers system security plans and rules of behavior.

changes.title: New family: System and Services Acquisition (SA)
changes.newText: System and Services Acquisition (SA) family added.
changes.rationale: Brings acquisition-time security (external system services, development requirements) into the CUI protection scope.

changes.title: New family: Supply Chain Risk Management (SR)
changes.newText: Supply Chain Risk Management (SR) family added.
changes.rationale: Reflects the SR family introduced in SP 800-53r5. Defense contractors must now address supply chain risk management plans and controls for CUI environments.

changes.title: Security Assessment family renamed 'Security Assessment and Monitoring'
changes.rationale: Rename reflects the inclusion of continuous monitoring within the family, consistent with SP 800-53r5.

changes.title: NFO tailoring category eliminated
changes.oldText: Controls marked NFO ('expected to be routinely satisfied by nonfederal organizations without specification') were excluded from the requirements.
changes.rationale: Per the NIST FAQ, feedback showed NFO controls — including foundational XX-1 policy-and-procedure controls — were simply not being implemented or assessed. Eliminating NFO moved several of those controls into the requirement set, increasing CUI, NCO, and FED assignments.

changes.title: Organization-defined parameters (ODPs) introduced — 88 parameters
changes.newText: Selected requirements now contain organization-defined parameters consistent with SP 800-53r5 (e.g., time periods, frequencies, thresholds). A new appendix consolidates all ODPs.
changes.rationale: Federal agencies can specify ODP values, provide guidance, or leave selection to the nonfederal organization; once set, ODP values become part of the assessable requirement. This replaces vague terms like "periodically."

changes.title: New tailoring criteria: Not Applicable (NA) and Other Related Control (ORC)
changes.newText: NA applies to the Program Management (PM) and PII Processing and Transparency (PT) families, which are not allocated to any 800-53B baseline. ORC marks controls whose protection capability is provided by another control.
changes.rationale: ORC eliminates redundancy in the requirement set without reducing protection; NA makes the tailoring analysis of the moderate baseline complete and transparent.

changes.title: Requirement specificity significantly increased
changes.oldText: Requirements stated at a high level of abstraction, leaving detailed specification to implementers and assessors.
changes.newText: Requirement language aligned with SP 800-53 control language, with discussion sections resequenced to match requirement parts.
changes.rationale: Per the NIST FAQ, the old abstraction made assessments inconsistent — assessors had different interpretations of what satisfied a requirement. Increased specificity narrows the interpretation gap.

changes.title: Numbering scheme changed: leading zeros and requirement titles
changes.newText: Requirements numbered 03.01.01, 03.01.02, … with a title for each requirement, hyperlinked references to SP 800-53 source controls, and CPRT dataset availability (spreadsheet and JSON).
changes.rationale: Supports automated tooling and aligns numbering with SP 800-171Ar3. Compliance tooling and SSP templates keyed to old numbering need remapping — NIST publishes transition mapping tables.

changes.title: Withdrawn requirements incorporated into multi-part requirements
changes.rationale: Closely related requirements were merged where a direct relationship exists, producing multi-part requirements consistent with SP 800-53 control structure. The change analysis and transition mapping tables published by NIST document each disposition.

changes.title: Assessment objectives increased from 320 to 390 (SP 800-171Ar3)
changes.newText: 390 assessment objectives in SP 800-171Ar3, restructured to align with SP 800-53A syntax, with source references added.
changes.rationale: Despite fewer requirements, there is more to assess — a practical signal that Rev 3 is a net increase in rigor, not a reduction.

changes.title: CUI overlay published
changes.newText: A CUI overlay shows how each control and control item in the SP 800-53B moderate baseline is tailored to produce the SP 800-171 requirements.
changes.rationale: Makes the derivation from the moderate baseline fully transparent for the first time — useful for organizations that run both 800-53 and 800-171 programs.

changes.title: ISO/IEC 27001 mapping table removed
changes.oldText: SP 800-171r2 included a mapping of security requirements to ISO/IEC 27001 controls.
changes.newText: Mapping tables focus exclusively on SP 800-53 source controls; NIST separately maintains the SP 800-53r5 to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 mapping.
changes.rationale: Organizations that relied on the in-document ISO crosswalk must now use the separate NIST mapping resources.

changes.title: STATUS — DoD/CMMC still assess against Rev 2
changes.rationale: The Department of Defense has stated that Revision 2 remains the compliance standard for contractors subject to DFARS 252.204-7012 (and CMMC assessments) until DoD announces otherwise. Track DFARS rulemaking for the Rev 3 transition timeline.
